nodes.title: Guard null and primitive values early

anchor.snippet: if (obj === null || obj === undefined) return false;

nodes.why: Only objects/functions have a prototype chain, so these quick checks avoid unnecessary work.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear and safe behavior, with a small upfront branching cost.

nodes.title: Validate constructor and read target prototype
nodes.why: `instanceof` semantics depend on a valid constructor prototype to compare against.
nodes.alternative: Skip checks and assume `Constructor` is always valid.
nodes.tradeoff: More defensive and predictable error handling, with a few extra lines.

nodes.title: Walk upward until match or chain end
nodes.why: This directly mirrors how `instanceof` works: compare each prototype link by reference.
nodes.tradeoff: Great for learning internals, but it does not cover custom `Symbol.hasInstance` behavior.
